Regeneron basks in first gene therapy approval as Dupixent, Eylea carry robust Q1 earnings
snippet: Regeneron hauled in $3.6 billion during the first quarter of 2026, as analysts homed in on a slight Eylea HD miss and key upcoming readouts, including for LAG3 candidate fianlimab in metastatic melanoma.

title: Biogen ready to catch Alzheimer’s patients transitioning off Lilly’s Kisunla
snippet: Key dosing differences between Eli Lilly’s Kisunla and Biogen’s Leqembi are about to come to a head in the Alzheimer's market as patients end their 18-month course of Lilly’s product.

title: Chiesi snaps up KalVista for $1.9B, betting big on rare disease med Ekterly
snippet: Chiesi Group is taking KalVista Pharmaceuticals under its wing, paying $1.9 billion for the biotech’s oral therapy Ekterly to treat severe swelling episodes caused by the rare genetic disorder hereditary angioedema.

title: AstraZeneca bullish on $80B sales goal after beating expectations, Phase 3 data wins
snippet: After a quarter in which sales topped $15 billion and key readouts went AstraZeneca’s way, the company is increasingly confident that its 2030 revenue target is in reach.

title: GSK’s Shingrix strategy shift drives sales beat even as vaccines slow
snippet: While sales for most of GSK’s shots slumped as vaccine skepticism continues to climb in the U.S., Shingrix jumped 20% to almost $1.4 billion in the first quarter, emerging as the pharma’s top-selling product.

title: Pfizer settles with generic drugmakers to protect blockbuster drug until 2031
snippet: The settlements delay the entry of generic copies of Pfizer’s Vyndamax by almost three years, stabilizing sales of a drug that generated $3.8 billion in the U.S. last year.

title: AstraZeneca faces uphill battle at upcoming adcomm in face of FDA questions
snippet: In briefing documents released Wednesday, the FDA raises doubts about two AstraZeneca assets set to be discussed Friday at the agency’s first drug-related advisory committee meeting in nine months.

title: Generic of Novo’s GLP-1 arrives in Canada, a ‘test case for the world’
snippet: Analysts will be watching as a generic version of semaglutide—marketed by Novo Nordisk as Wegovy for weight loss—launches in Canada as a test case for future price erosion in the U.S.

title: Amgen, AstraZeneca join FDA effort harnessing cloud for real-time clinical trials
snippet: The FDA’s real-time clinical trial mechanism allows drug sponsors to transmit data immediately to the regulator through the cloud—a system that could “compress drug development timelines,” Jefferies analysts said.

title: Novartis, PTC push Huntington’s drug into Phase 3, mum on accelerated approval
snippet: Phase 2 data from PTC Therapeutics showed that the Novartis-partnered Huntington’s disease asset slowed progression by more than 50%. Analysts say the decision to initiate a last-stage trial reflects a lack of confidence in an accelerated FDA nod.

title: Q1 earnings take off, Lilly strikes many deals, Regeneron notches historic approval, FDA raises questions
snippet: Sanofi and Novartis kick off the heart of earnings season; Lilly strikes its fourth pact in as many weeks; Regeneron earns landmark approval for a gene therapy for a type of genetic deafness, and also strikes a White House deal; FDA asks Amgen to withdraw Tavneos and, separately, issues Commissioner’s National Priorit...

title: Biotech angels lean into deeper diligence and disciplined early bets
snippet: Angel investors are raising the bar with tighter criteria, backing strong teams, realistic markets and clear paths to exit while applying deeper, domain-specific diligence to early stage bets.

title: Altitude Labs serves up higher education for biotech founders
snippet: Altitude Labs, an offshoot of AI-focused techbio Recursion, is teaching scientists to build companies, one founder at a time.

title: Lilly can’t stop the dealing, with nearly $21B spent on M&A this year—so far
snippet: With six acquisitions already this year, Eli Lilly’s business development shows no signs of stopping as executives make good on a promise to spend their GLP-1 gains.

title: Patient advocate offers Recursion a powerful reason to keep going in rare disease
snippet: Staff at Salt Lake City-based techbio company Recursion recently heard from Jenny and Tim Jones about their challenging family history of familial adenomatous polyposis.

title: Exclusive: Supabase Execs Were So Impressed With Dreambase, They Became Investors In Its $3.7M Round

Persistent inflammation induced by various risk factors is the main cause of GBC, however, the molecular program remains elusive. title: Efficacy of selpercatinib as a first-line treatment for-fusion positive non-small-cell lung cancer: a novel two-stage Bayesian network meta-analysis.
snippet: Single-arm trial data is frequently used during the reimbursement of new oncology interventions. Evaluating treatment effects relative to multiple relevant comparators via network meta-analysis (NMA) using data from single-arm trials; however, remains a challenge. title: AbbVie sinks talons into KRAS with right to buy Kestrel for up to $1.45B
snippet: AbbVie is setting up a shot to buy Kestrel Therapeutics down the line, as the biotech doses patients in a Phase 1 trial for the oral pan-KRAS inhibitor KST-6051 in solid tumors.

title: Boehringer, Zealand’s obesity drug ‘more akin’ to Novo’s Wegovy in Phase 3
snippet: Topline Phase 3 results of survodutide—licensed to Boehringer Ingelheim from Zealand Pharma—are more comparable to Novo Nordisk’s Wegovy than to Eli Lilly’s Zepbound, William Blair analysts said on Tuesday.

title: AACR 2026: Combo therapies by Moderna, Marengo show promise in skin, breast cancer
snippet: Cancer cocktails pairing Moderna’s mRNA-4359 with Merck’s Keytruda and Marengo’s invikafusp alfa with Gilead Sciences’ Trodelvy showed promising results, while a complex combination by Agenus and MiNK Therapeutics failed to elicit an overall response.

title: Novartis cuts hit New Jersey again, affecting 60 employees
snippet: Follow along as BioSpace tracks job cuts and restructuring initiatives.

title: Lilly, AI biotech Profluent ink $2.25B pact in search of genetic medicine ‘holy grail’
snippet: Weight loss giant Eli Lilly has penned a DNA editing deal with Profluent Bio, continuing its dealmaking spree as well as its commitment to artificial intelligence.

title: FXR-YAP signalling maintains biliary epithelial cell identity and preserves liver homeostasis.
snippet: Bile acids (BAs) flow through ducts lined by biliary epithelial cells (BECs), which preserve ductal integrity and liver homeostasis. Failure of this barrier causes BA accumulation in the liver parenchyma, and ultimately fibrosis. snippet: Extracellular vesicles (EVs) are small nanometric particles surrounded by a lipid bilayer and actively secreted by different cell types. title: Biotech has become a good news story. Let the sun shine
snippet: Doubling survival in pancreatic cancer, a long-fought rare disease approval, a massive IPO and ambitious biotech entrepreneurs have BioSpace Senior Editor Annalee Armstrong feeling upbeat about the biotech scene.

title: Ferroptosis in Breast Cancer: Molecular Insights and Therapeutic Strategies.
snippet: Ferroptosis is a regulated form of cell death driven by iron-dependent lipid peroxidation and insufficient antioxidant defenses and is mechanistically distinct from apoptosis, necroptosis, and other cell death mechanisms. title: Multi-omics evidence reveals a causal role of endoplasmic reticulum stress in cancer development.

Cancer-associated fibroblasts (CAFs) are a core matrix component of TIME. However, their functional heterogeneity and the specific molecular mechanisms driving tumor progression have not been fully elucidated. In addition, the role of nuc...

title: Transcriptomic profiling and targeted validation reveal molecular mechanisms of oxygen therapy in high-altitude cerebral injury.
snippet: Exposure to high-altitude hypoxia is associated with an increased risk of impaired brain structure and function, with oxidative stress and neuroinflammation widely recognized as key mechanisms involved. title: RNA-binding motif proteins as context-dependent regulators of tumor-immune crosstalk, genome stability, and therapeutic vulnerabilities in cancer.
snippet: Cancer progression relies on dynamic post-transcriptional RNA regulation to enable phenotypic plasticity, immune evasion, and therapeutic resistance. title: CXCR4-Targeted Nanotherapeutics: A Promising Approach for Liver Fibrosis and Hepatocellular Carcinoma Management.

title: Case Report: durable response to pulsed electric field ablation in combination with immune checkpoint inhibitors in HER2-low breast cancer through activation of interferon signaling.
snippet: Pulsed Electric Field (PEF) ablation is a non-thermal cancer treatment that disrupts tumor cell membranes while preserving the immune microenvironment and promoting immunogenic cell death. title: Case report: immunotherapy response in pancreatic cancer after MSI-H emerged post-chemotherapy.
snippet: Microsatellite instability-high (MSI-H) is an established biomarker of response to immune checkpoint inhibitors, though it is rarely observed in pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC). title: Case Report: Successful treatment of mismatch repair-deficient cervical esophageal adenocarcinoma with immune checkpoint inhibition.
snippet: Cervical (upper) esophageal adenocarcinoma is an extremely rare, aggressive, and biologically distinct subtype of esophageal cancer, with few cases reported in literature. title: Immune checkpoint inhibition inassociated gastric cancer: a case report highlighting the role of biomarker-driven treatment selection.
snippet: Gastroesophageal adenocarcinoma (GEA) represents a heterogenous disease with poor prognosis, in which biomarker-driven strategies have gained relevance to optimize treatment selection. title: Integrative analysis of a novel snoRNA-based prognostic signature in patients with breast cancer.
snippet: Breast cancer (BC) is the most commonly diagnosed malignancy in women worldwide. Prognostic heterogeneity driven by molecular subtypes and tumor microenvironment underscores the need for novel biomarkers. Small nucleolar RNAs (snoRNAs) have emerged as potential regulators in cancer biology, but their prognostic value in BC remains unclear. We analyzed snoRN...

title: Dynamics of the immune repertoire in recurrent, locally advanced NSCLC not amenable for definitive therapy and in stage IV disease receiving first-line chemotherapy.
snippet: Cytotoxic chemotherapy can modulate antitumor immunity, yet its impact on the peripheral T-cell receptor (TCR) repertoire in non-targetable advanced NSCLC remains poorly characterized. We prospectively investigated chemotherapy-induced TCRβ repertoire dynamics and their prognostic relevance. Patients with recurrent unresectable locally advanced or sta...

title: Comprehensive profiling of alternative splicing and immune landscapes in rectal cancer: implications for mRNA vaccine design and immune subtype stratification.
snippet: mRNA vaccines have emerged as a promising platform for cancer immunotherapy, particularly following the success of COVID-19 vaccines. However, the development of cancer vaccines presents challenges such as difficulties in antigen prediction and poor immunogenicity, especially in identifying and delivering highly immunogenic tumor-specific antigens. title: An Improved Deep Learning Algorithm for Breast Cancer Survival Prediction Based on Multi-Omics Data.
snippet: Breast cancer is a leading cause of mortality among women worldwide. Accurate survival prediction can improve clinical decision-making and support personalized treatment planning. This study aims to develop an interpretable and effective deep learning model for breast cancer survival prediction using multi-omics data. This study proposes a novel deep learni...
